title: 7 Basic Shipping Description On Hazmat Shipping Paper
source_filename: 7 Basic Shipping Description On Hazmat Shipping Paper.docx

qas.q: basic shipping description includes what four items found in the HMT
qas.a: proper shipping name, hazard class or division, identification number, packing group

when transported in bulk packaging is one of the following, liquid phase at a temperature greater than 100 degrees Celsius. In a liquid phase of the flashpoint of 37.8 degrees C. In the solid phase at a temperature greater than 240 degrees Celsius

the maximum weight or volume of a hazardous material that can be transported in a single bowl for non-book package. This weight or volume does not include the package itself. Has mats that are transported as part of a device include the weight of the entire device

a paragraph that must be included on the shipping paper indicating the hazardous material being shipped has been prepared packaged and labeled in accordance with all the HMR requirements for the mode of transportation being used. The shipper certifications are found in 172.2 04 and are different for each of the four modes of transportation

qas.q: Shipping paper documents contain the following information
qas.a: a basic description of the material being transported. A description of the total quantity being transported. An indication of whether or not the material is considered hazardous. A description of the number and type of packages being transported.
document issued by the shipper to a carrier that describes the material to be shipped and certifies that the shipment has been properly prepared according to the HMR

qas.q: shipping papers must accompany the shipment and must include
qas.a: the basic description of the hazardous material, total quantity including the unit of measure, shipper certification statement, shipper signature, emergency information

qas.q: contents of a Hazmat Shipping paper
qas.a: identification number from column for, proper shipping name from column 2, primary hazard class and division from home three, subsidiary hazard class and division if any from calling 6, packing group in Roman numerals from column five. The emergency response telephone number

must be entered on The Shipping paper immediately following the description of the hazardous material or entered once on The Shipping paper and then clearly visible location. Must be monitored at all times that hazardous materials in transportation. The number must be to someone knowledgeable about the hazardous material being shipped and has comprehensive emergency response an incident Mana gation information or has immediate access to a person who possesses such knowledge and information.

qas.q: only in the case of hazardous waste is there what
qas.a: they prescribed Shipping paper format, the uniform hazardous waste manifest. Otherwise there is no specific form for hazardous material Shipping paper

qas.q: a super may use a form specific to its operation as long as
qas.a: The Shipping paper description has six items in the proper order. The basic description, proper shipping name, hazard class division number, identification number, and packing group in that order. Identification number, proper shipping name, hazard class and division number, and packing group in that order. Next the total quantity material last the number and type of packages being shipped

qas.q: when hazardous materials and non-hazardous materials are entered on The Shipping paper, that hazardous materials must
qas.a: be entered first, be highlighted, be identified with the letter X in the column caption to hm,

qas.q: shipments that do not require description of hazardous material on Shipping paper
qas.a: identified by the letter a in column 1 except when the material is offered or intended for transportation by air. Identified by the letter W and call them one except one which was offered her intended for transportation by water. A limited quantity package unless the material is offered for transportation by air or vessel. Orm-d material when offered for transportation by Highway rail or vessel. Category be infectious substances. none of the exceptions apply of the material is a hazardous waste hazardous substance or Marine pollutant
